problem_frame: The Uigwe docs now need a wrapper-facing surface, but the wrapper should sit on top of the existing protocol rather than mutate the planner core.

selected_approach.title: Documentation-first wrapper contract
selected_approach.summary: Define the wrapper entry, output contract, and boundary rules in docs first so implementation can follow a stable protocol surface later.
pros: Fits the current documentation-first state of the project
Keeps planner and wrapper responsibilities separate
cons: Does not produce an immediately runnable wrapper

alternatives.title: Documentation-first wrapper contract
alternatives.summary: Define the wrapper entry, output contract, and boundary rules in docs first so implementation can follow a stable protocol surface later.

alternatives.title: Implement a wrapper skill immediately
alternatives.summary: Jump straight to a wrapper skill without documenting the consumer-facing interface first.
cons: High risk of locking in the wrong contract
Would mix protocol design with surface implementation

alternatives.title: Keep everything inside PROTOCOL.md only
alternatives.summary: Avoid a dedicated wrapper surface and extend the protocol doc with invocation guidance.
cons: Weak separation between planner core and wrapper surface

tradeoffs: The selected approach favors contract stability over immediate execution.
The rejected skill-first path is faster in the short term but risks hardcoding premature assumptions.
The retained protocol-only alternative is simpler, but weaker as a future integration surface.
key_decisions: Do not mutate packet or goal-tree contracts as part of wrapper planning.
Treat the wrapper as a consumer-facing layer on top of Uigwe, not part of the planner core.
Document boundaries first, then implementation later.
assumptions: The current Uigwe docs are stable enough to support a dedicated wrapper document.
A wrapper will eventually need a formal input/output contract.

risks.description: The wrapper spec may accidentally duplicate or redefine protocol responsibilities.
risks.mitigation: Keep explicit planner vs wrapper boundary text in the draft.

risks.description: Adding wrapper docs may fragment the Uigwe package if linked poorly.
risks.mitigation: Update the docs index and cross-link protocol and wrapper surfaces.

validation_plan: Check that wrapper docs do not redefine protocol artifacts.
Verify that the proposed wrapper surface is linkable from the existing Uigwe docs index.
Review whether a future implementer could build the wrapper from the documented contract.
